docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Sana-1.5 1.6B (20 steps) vs Sana-Sprint 1.6B (2 steps) — same prompt, same seed. "a majestic rubber duck wearing aviator goggles, perched on a futuristic motorcycle in a cyberpunk neon-lit city, raining"

sana_video_generate — 5s sunrise · 2s cyberpunk teaser, 480p, generated on NVIDIA Thor

docs.title: strands-sana
docs.text: NVIDIA Sana inference + training as Strands Agent tools. 33 tools · 15 models · 7 pipelines · MP4 video · LoRA · ControlNet · Sol-RL.

docs.title: Why strands-sana?
docs.text:
pip install strands-sana

from strands import Agent
from strands_sana import sana_generate, sana_video_generate

agent = Agent(tools=[sana_generate, sana_video_generate])
agent("Generate a cyberpunk cityscape at 1024px, then animate it as a 5s clip")

That's it. The agent picks the right model, sets sane defaults, downloads weights on first use, and saves outputs. All 33 tools work the same way.

docs.title: How it fits together
docs.text:
graph LR
    User([🗣️ Agent prompt]) --> Tools

    subgraph Tools[" 33 @tool functions "]
        T2I["🖼️ sana_generate<br/>sana_sprint_generate<br/>sana_inpaint<br/>sana_controlnet_generate"]
        IMG["🎨 sana_img2img"]
        VID["🎬 sana_video_generate<br/>sana_image_to_video"]
        TRAIN["🎓 sana_train_lora<br/>sana_train_solrl<br/>sana_train_video<br/>+4 more"]
        EXTRAS["⚙️ schedulers · quant · LoRA<br/>metrics · ComfyUI export<br/>HF upload · prefetch"]
    end

    Tools --> Wrapper["SanaPipelineWrapper<br/>(7 pipeline kinds)"]
    Wrapper --> Diffusers["🧨 diffusers ≥ 0.32"]
    Wrapper --> Upstream["📚 NVlabs/Sana<br/>(training scripts)"]
    Diffusers --> GPU([⚡ CUDA / MPS / CPU])

    style Tools fill:#5e35b1,stroke:#333,color:#fff
    style Wrapper fill:#7e57c2,stroke:#333,color:#fff
    style Diffusers fill:#ec407a,stroke:#333,color:#fff

Inference path uses diffusers directly — fast, well-maintained, GPU-accelerated. Training path shells out to upstream NVlabs/Sana scripts (auto-discovered).

docs.title: What you can do
docs.text:
• 🖼️ Generate images

    Sana 1.0 / 1.5 / Sprint, plus PAG, Inpainting, ControlNet, multi-image batches.

    → Image generation

  • 🎬 Generate videos

    SANA-Video 480p / 720p / LongSANA. Text-to-video and image-to-video.

    → Video generation

  • ⚡ Sana-Sprint

    1-2 step distilled inference. ~0.1s per 1024px image on H100.

    → Sprint

  • 🎨 Image-to-image

    Restyle existing images at Sprint speed.

    → Img2Img

  • 🎓 Train your own

    LoRA, full pretrain, sCM-LADD distillation, Sol-RL post-training, video FSDP.

    → Training

  • 🔬 Inference scaling

    Generate K candidates, pick best by CLIP / NVILA.

    → Inference scaling

  • ⚙️ Memory modes

    Run 1.6B in 8 GB VRAM via int4/int8 quantization + offload.

    → Memory & quantization

  • 🔌 Schedulers

    10 alias (flow-match-euler, DPM-Solver, Euler, DDIM, …) — swap at runtime.

    → Schedulers

  • 📊 Metrics

    CLIPScore, ImageReward — score generations programmatically.

    → Metrics

docs.title: Tested on real hardware
docs.text:
11 bugs were caught and fixed by stress-testing every tool on an NVIDIA Thor dev kit (CUDA 13). See the full bug log.

→ Continue to Quickstart or browse the Gallery.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: All 33 @tool functions exposed by strands-sana. Auto-callable, fully typed.

docs.title: sana_generate
docs.text: Primary text-to-image. Auto-dispatches to PAG when pag_scale > 0. → See Image Generation

docs.title: sana_batch(prompts, ...)
docs.text: One image per prompt; same defaults as sana_generate.

docs.title: sana_load_model(model="list", kind=None, tag=None)
docs.text: List or pre-load a checkpoint. Supports kind= and tag= filters. → See Models

docs.title: sana_sprint_generate
docs.text: 1-2 step distilled inference. → See Sana-Sprint

docs.title: sana_inpaint(prompt, image_path, mask_path, ...)
docs.text: Text-guided inpainting (auto-detects SanaInpaintPipeline in diffusers).

docs.title: sana_controlnet_generate
docs.text: HED/Canny/depth-guided generation. → See ControlNet

docs.title: sana_img2img
docs.text: Sana-Sprint image-to-image restyle. → See Img2Img

docs.title: sana_inference_scale(prompt, n_samples, score_fn="clip")
docs.text: Generate K candidates, pick best by CLIP. → See Inference Scaling

docs.title: sana_video_generate
docs.text: Text-to-video. 4 models, default 480p × 121 frames @ 24fps. → See Video Generation

docs.title: sana_image_to_video(image_path, prompt, ...)
docs.text: Animate a still image into a video clip.

docs.title: sana_load_lora(repo_or_path, scale=1.0, adapter_name=None, model=None)
docs.text: Load LoRA weights. → See LoRA

docs.title: sana_unload_loras(model=None)
docs.text: Remove all LoRA adapters.

docs.title: sana_set_memory_mode(mode="balanced", model=None)
docs.text: Modes: fast · balanced · low · ultra-low. → See Memory & Quantization

docs.title: sana_clear_cache()
docs.text: Drop loaded pipelines, free GPU memory.

docs.title: sana_set_scheduler(name, model=None, use_flow_sigmas=True)
docs.text: Swap the active scheduler. 10 aliases. → See Schedulers

docs.title: sana_list_schedulers()
docs.text: List available aliases.

docs.title: sana_quantize(bits=8, backend="auto", model=None)
docs.text: Quantize the active pipeline. Tries Quanto / bnb / Nunchaku in order.

docs.title: sana_swap_vae(vae_repo, model=None)
docs.text: Replace the VAE (e.g. with DC-AE-Lite for faster decode).

docs.title: sana_metric_clip(image_path, prompt)
docs.text: CLIPScore between an image and a prompt.

docs.title: sana_metric_imagereward(image_path, prompt)
docs.text: ImageReward score (requires pip install image-reward).

docs.title: sana_enhance_prompt(prompt, style="photorealistic")
docs.text: Local style hint. Styles: photorealistic, anime, oil-painting, cinematic, minimalist.

docs.title: sana_export_comfyui_workflow(prompt, output_path, ...)
docs.text: Emit ComfyUI graph JSON. → See ComfyUI Export

docs.title: sana_safety_check(prompt)
docs.text: Keyword-filter NSFW/CSAM gate. ShieldGemma upgrade path documented.

docs.title: sana_upload_to_hf(path, repo_id, repo_type="model")
docs.text: Push local artifacts (LoRA weights, generated images) to HuggingFace.

docs.title: sana_serve(model="sana-1.6b-1024", port=30000, ...)
docs.text: Spawn an SGLang inference server.

docs.title: sana_prefetch_model(model="sana-1.6b-1024", quiet=False)
docs.text: Pre-download a checkpoint with progress reporting.

docs.title: sana_train_lora(instance_data_dir, ...)
docs.text: DreamBooth + LoRA fine-tuning. Wraps upstream train_dreambooth_lora_sana.py.

docs.title: sana_train(config_path, ...)
docs.text: Full pretrain / finetune (FSDP / DDP).

docs.title: sana_train_scm_ladd(config_path, ...)
docs.text: Sana-Sprint sCM-LADD distillation.

docs.title: sana_train_solrl(config_spec, ...)
docs.text: Sol-RL post-training (NVFP4 rollout + BF16 optimization).

docs.title: sana_train_video(config_path, chunk=False, ...)
docs.text: SANA-Video FSDP training.

docs.title: sana_train_longsana(config_path, ...)
docs.text: LongSANA real-time minute-long video training.

docs.title: sana_list_training_configs(sana_root=None)
docs.text:
Enumerate all upstream YAML/PY configs (~38 of them).

→ Full training guide: Training

docs.title: strands_sana.pipeline.SanaPipelineWrapper
docs.text: The class behind every inference tool. Supports kind dispatch, LoRA, memory modes, scheduler swap.

docs.title: strands_sana.pipeline.make_step_callback(every_n=5, on_step=None)
docs.text: Build a callback_on_step_end for streaming preview decodes.

docs.title: strands_sana.models.list_models(kind=None, tag=None)
docs.text: Programmatic registry filter.

docs.title: strands_sana.utils.io.save_image / load_image
docs.text: PIL helpers; load_image accepts file path / URL / bytes.

docs.title: strands_sana.utils.prompts.COMPLEX_HUMAN_INSTRUCTION
docs.text: The official Sana Gemma-2 prompt-expansion template.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: strands-sana is a thin agent-tool wrapper. The hard work is delegated to diffusers (inference) and upstream NVlabs/Sana scripts (training).

docs.title: Module layout
docs.text:
strands_sana/
├── __init__.py             # Public API: 33 @tool functions
├── tools/
│   ├── generate.py         # 13 core inference tools
│   ├── extras.py           # 10 extras (schedulers, quant, metrics, …)
│   ├── video.py            # 2 video tools
│   ├── img2img.py          # 1 img2img tool
│   └── training.py         # 7 training tools (subprocess to NVlabs/Sana)
├── pipeline/
│   └── sana_pipeline.py    # SanaPipelineWrapper — kind dispatch + caching
├── models/
│   └── registry.py         # 15 SanaModelInfo entries, list/filter helpers
└── utils/
    ├── io.py               # save_image, load_image, ensure_output_dir
    └── prompts.py          # COMPLEX_HUMAN_INSTRUCTION + style helpers

docs.title: Inference path
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User / Agent
    participant T as @tool function
    participant W as SanaPipelineWrapper
    participant D as diffusers
    participant G as GPU

    U->>T: sana_generate(prompt=..., model=...)
    T->>W: get_pipeline(model_name, kind_override)
    W->>W: cache lookup
    alt cache miss
        W->>D: from_pretrained(hf_repo, dtype=bf16)
        D->>G: load weights
        W->>W: enable_model_cpu_offload()
    end
    T->>W: pipe.generate(prompt, steps, seed, ...)
    W->>D: pipe(**kwargs)
    D->>G: forward pass × N steps
    D-->>W: PIL.Image[]
    W-->>T: list[PIL.Image]
    T->>T: save_image(...)
    T-->>U: {"path": "...", "model": "...", ...}

docs.title: Pipeline kinds
docs.text:
graph TB
    Wrapper["SanaPipelineWrapper"]
    Wrapper --> T2I["t2i → SanaPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> PAG["pag → SanaPAGPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> Sprint["sprint → SanaSprintPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> SprintI2I["sprint-i2i → SanaSprintImg2ImgPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> Ctrl["controlnet → SanaControlNetPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> Vid["video → SanaVideoPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> I2V["image-to-video → SanaImageToVideoPipeline"]

    style Wrapper fill:#5e35b1,stroke:#333,color:#fff

The kind is determined by:

  1. kind_override argument (explicit)
  2. SanaModelInfo.pipeline_kind (model registry)
  3. pag_scale > 0 upgrade (only for t2i → pag)

docs.title: Cache key
docs.text:
key = f"{model_name}::{kind}::{device}"

So (sana-1.6b-1024, t2i, cuda) and (sana-1.6b-1024, pag, cuda) are separate entries — switching just flips the active pipeline class without re-downloading weights (where shared).

docs.title: Training path
docs.text:
Training is fundamentally different — we shell out:

sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User / Agent
    participant T as sana_train_*
    participant R as resolve_sana_root()
    participant FS as Filesystem
    participant SP as subprocess
    participant SC as NVlabs/Sana script

    U->>T: sana_train_lora(instance_data_dir=...)
    T->>R: find Sana/ via cwd / SANA_ROOT / package
    R->>FS: stat ./Sana/train_scripts
    FS-->>R: ✓
    T->>T: build [accelerate, launch, ...] argv
    alt dry_run=True
        T-->>U: {"command": "...", "dry_run": true}
    else dry_run=False
        T->>SP: Popen(argv, cwd=Sana, env={...})
        SP->>SC: torchrun / accelerate ...
        T-->>U: {"pid": 12345, "command": "..."}
    end

Auto-resolves Sana/ via:

  1. Explicit sana_root= argument
  2. SANA_ROOT env var
  3. ./Sana relative to cwd
  4. Package-relative ../../Sana

docs.title: Public API surface
docs.text: 33 @tool functions divided into 6 groups — see API Reference.

docs.title: What we don't do
docs.text:
• We don't vendor NVlabs/Sana research code — diffusers carries the inference; upstream Sana repo carries training scripts
  • We don't reimplement scheduling — pulled from diffusers
  • We don't ship checkpoints — auto-downloaded from HuggingFace on first use

This keeps the package small and ensures every Sana feature lands here as soon as upstream ships it.

docs.title: Bugs found & fixed via real-world testing on Thor
docs.text:
Tested on NVIDIA Thor dev kit (aarch64, sm_110, CUDA 13).

# Severity Bug Fix Commit 1 medium Test scaffolding _call(t, ...) pattern using .original_func doesn't exist on real Strands DecoratedFunctionTool Tests already had else t(**kw) fallback path n/a 2 low training tests fail when no Sana/ repo present (cross-machine portability) Auto-clone shallow Sana into /tmp/_sana_for_tests via session fixture 59a0a34 3 critical negative_prompt or None → AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'lower' when guidance_scale > 1.0 Use empty string "" not None 59a0a34 4 critical All 5 video model HF repos are 404 (made-up names) Use canonical names from model_zoo.md: Sana-Video_2B_480p_diffusers, SANA-Video_2B_720p_diffusers, SANA-Video_2B_480p_LongLive_diffusers a0a665c 5 critical Sana-Sprint with steps != 2 → ValueError: Intermediate timesteps for SCM is not supported when num_inference_steps != 2 Pass intermediate_timesteps=None when steps≠2 891b91b 6 critical SANA-Video errors on (h, w) not divisible by 32; binning gave 624×624 Snap to 32, disable use_resolution_binning for video e2921d5 7 low MP4 export silently falls back to WebP without imageio-ffmpeg Multi-tier fallback + [video] extra c9f2472 8 low Test helper name= kwarg collides with sana_set_scheduler(name=...) (test bug, not lib bug) — switched to scp-file approach n/a 9 critical Schedulers like DDIM/Heun reject prediction_type=flow_prediction from Sana's flow-matching scheduler config Strip flow keys when swapping to non-flow schedulers, with quality-degradation warning 9ea3421 10 medium ControlNet with non-ControlNet checkpoint → cryptic component error Catch and return helpful error pointing to upstream app/sana_controlnet_pipeline.py b4c71ec 11 medium sana_load_lora propagates ValueError: PEFT backend is required and RepositoryNotFoundError instead of returning error dict Wrap entire body in try/except BaseException, handle PEFT-missing specially. Add [lora] extra e91df63, f81c540

docs.title: Image generation
docs.text: Test Time Result sana_generate (sana-0.6b-512, 10 steps) 45.8s ✅ PNG 512×512 sana_generate PAG (pag_scale=2.5) 29.3s ✅ kind=pag sana_generate complex_instruction 34.8s ✅ Gemma-2 prompt expansion sana_generate Sana-1.5 1.6B 163s ✅ best-quality 1024×1024 sana_generate num_images=2 40.3s ✅ 2 PNGs sana_sprint_generate (0.6B, 2 step) 48.9s ✅ PNG 1024 sana_sprint_generate (0.6B, 4 step) 43.0s ✅ steps≠2 works sana_img2img (sprint-i2i 1.6B, strength 0.7) 9.3s ✅ PNG 1024 sana_img2img strength 0.3 77.5s ✅ PNG 1024

docs.title: Video generation
docs.text: Test Time Result sana_video_generate (480p, 33 frames, 10 steps) 75.6s ✅ MP4 (1.4s clip) sana_video_generate 65 frames 89.0s ✅ MP4 (2.7s) sana_video_generate 97 frames 102.6s ✅ MP4 (4.0s) sana_image_to_video (33 frames, 8 steps) 120.6s ✅ MP4

docs.title: Schedulers (all 6 swap successfully)
docs.text: Scheduler Gen time flow-match-euler (default) 48.1s dpm-solver 13.3s euler 6.2s euler-ancestral 6.3s ddim 7.0s deis 7.1s heun 7.7s

docs.title: Other tools
docs.text: Test Time Result sana_export_comfyui_workflow 0.0s ✅ JSON for 4 model variants sana_safety_check (safe + bad + unicode + empty) 0.0s ✅ all branches sana_inference_scale (3 samples + CLIP) 15.8s ✅ best=index 1 sana_inference_scale (n=2, first) 31.6s ✅ sana_metric_clip 1.8s ✅ score=0.254 sana_load_model("list", kind=, tag=) 0.0s ✅ sana_set_memory_mode low 35.4s ✅ sana_prefetch_model (0.6B-512) 14.3s ✅ sana_quantize (no backend) 31.8s ✅ status=deferred sana_load_lora (bogus repo) 27.3s ✅ status=error sana_unload_loras 0.0s ✅

docs.title: Training (dry-run, all 6)
docs.text: Tool Result sana_list_training_configs ✅ 38 configs sana_train_lora ✅ accelerate launch ... sana_train ✅ torchrun ... sana_train_video ✅ torchrun video ... sana_train_video chunk=True ✅ chunked variant sana_train_solrl ✅ bash sol_rl/run_sana ... sana_train_scm_ladd ✅ torchrun scm_ladd ... sana_train_longsana ✅ torchrun longsana ...

docs.title: Pytest
docs.text: 72 passed in 6.30s

docs.title: Total
docs.text:
• 11 bugs found via real-world testing on Thor (5 critical, 4 medium, 2 low)
  • All fixed, all pushed
  • 9 fix commits on top of v0.4.0
  • 72 tests still passing
  • 17 example variants validated against real GPU
  • 3 video MP4s generated at 33/65/97 frames
  • Coverage: image, video, img2img, PAG, Sprint, schedulers, scaling, training dry-runs, LoRA, ControlNet, prompt enhance, safety, metrics, ComfyUI export

docs.title: Gallery
docs.text: Every asset on this page was generated by strands-sana itself on an NVIDIA Thor dev kit (sm_110, CUDA 13).

docs.title: Hero — Sana 1.5 vs Sana-Sprint
docs.text:
Same prompt, same seed, vastly different cost.

Sana-1.5 1.6B20 steps · 6.4s

Sana-Sprint 1.6B2 steps · 0.6s 
sana_generate(prompt=PROMPT, model="sana-1.5-1.6b-1024", steps=20, seed=42)
sana_sprint_generate(prompt=PROMPT, model="sana-sprint-1.6b-1024", steps=2, seed=42)

docs.title: PAG — Perturbed Attention Guidance
docs.text:
pag_scale=2.0 boosts coherence at ~2× compute cost.

sana_generate(prompt=PROMPT, model="sana-1.5-1.6b-1024",
              pag_scale=2.0, steps=15, seed=42)

docs.title: Img2Img — restyle anything
docs.text:
Source image (left) restyled with two different prompts.

Source (Sana-1.5)

"anime cel-shaded style"strength=0.5

"oil painting, masterpiece"strength=0.6 
sana_img2img(prompt="anime cel-shaded style",
             image_path="hero.png",
             strength=0.5, steps=2)

docs.title: Schedulers — same prompt, 4 schedulers
docs.text:
Sana is a flow-matching model, so flow-match-euler (default) is highest fidelity. DPM-Solver also works well; non-flow schedulers degrade quality but generate quickly.

flow-match-euler(default · best fidelity)

dpm-solver(13× faster)

euler(lossy on flow models)

ddim(lossy on flow models) 
sana_set_scheduler(name="dpm-solver", model="sana-0.6b-1024")
sana_generate(prompt=PROMPT, ...)

docs.title: Inference Scaling — 4 candidates, CLIP picks best
docs.text:
Generate N variants in parallel, score with CLIP, return the highest-aligned image.

seed 200CLIP 0.431

seed 201CLIP 0.453

seed 202 — winnerCLIP 0.462

seed 203CLIP 0.459 
sana_inference_scale(prompt=PROMPT, n_samples=4,
                     score_fn="clip", seed_start=200)

docs.title: Image-to-Video
docs.text:
Promote a still into a 49-frame clip.

Source PNG

49 frames · 24 fps 
sana_image_to_video(image_path="hero.png",
                    prompt="zoom out, neon flickers",
                    frames=49, steps=10)

→ Full MP4

docs.title: SANA-Video — 3 aesthetics
docs.text:
Ocean waves crashing on cliffs Watercolor fox running through autumn Neon plasma swirling 
for prompt in [...]:
    sana_video_generate(prompt=prompt, model="sana-video-2b-480",
                        frames=49, steps=10)

docs.title: Performance on Thor
docs.text:
Tool Model Output Time sana_generate sana-0.6b-512 512×512 PNG 45.8s sana_generate PAG sana-1.5-1.6b-1024 1024×1024 62.6s sana_generate (Sana-1.5) sana-1.5-1.6b-1024 1024×1024 163s sana_sprint_generate sana-sprint-0.6b-1024 1024×1024 48.9s sana_img2img sana-sprint-i2i-1.6b-1024 1024×1024 9.3s sana_video_generate (33 frames) sana-video-2b-480 480p MP4 75.6s sana_video_generate (97 frames) sana-video-2b-480 480p MP4 102.6s sana_image_to_video sana-video-i2v-480 480p MP4 74.8s sana_inference_scale (4×CLIP) sana-0.6b-1024 4 PNGs + winner 42.2s

Times include first-load model loading (~22s for 0.6B, ~52s for 2B video).

docs.title: Best of K
docs.text:
Generate K candidates, pick the best by CLIP, optionally animate it.

from strands_sana import sana_inference_scale, sana_image_to_video

# 1. Generate 8 candidates with same prompt, 8 different seeds
result = sana_inference_scale(
    prompt="a futuristic robot in a Japanese garden",
    model="sana-1.6b-1024",
    n_samples=8,
    seed_start=1000,
    score_fn="clip",
    steps=15,
    output_dir="./out",
)

print(f"Best: seed={result['best']['seed']} score={result['scores'][result['best']['index']]['score']:.3f}")

# 2. Animate the winner
sana_image_to_video(
    image_path=result["best"]["path"],
    prompt="slow zoom out, leaves rustle in the breeze",
    model="sana-video-i2v-480",
    frames=49, steps=10,
    output_dir="./out",
)

docs.title: Why best-of-K works
docs.text: CLIP scoring is cheap (~0.5s per image), generation is the expensive part. Burning 8× compute on candidates and picking the winner often beats one careful inference.

docs.title: Cost vs benefit
docs.text: Approach Compute Quality Single Sana-1.5 4.8B (20 steps) 1× High Best-of-4 with Sana-1.6B 4× Often higher Best-of-16 with Sana-Sprint ~1.6× Variable

docs.title: Variations
docs.text:
• score_fn="first" — skip scoring, useful for batch generation
  • Custom scorer: monkeypatch _clip_score in extras.py for your own ranker
  • Combine with PAG: sana_inference_scale(..., pag_scale=2.0) (after wiring)

docs.title: Multi-Style Batch
docs.text:
Generate the same scene in different aesthetic styles using sana_img2img.

from strands_sana import sana_generate, sana_img2img, sana_clear_cache

# 1. Establish a base image
base = sana_generate(
    prompt="a tranquil cabin in a misty forest at dawn",
    model="sana-1.6b-1024", steps=20, seed=42,
)
src = base["path"]

# 2. Restyle 4 different ways with Sprint Img2Img (much cheaper)
sana_clear_cache()
styles = [
    ("oil-painting",   "oil painting, thick brushstrokes, masterpiece"),
    ("anime",          "anime cel-shaded style, vibrant colors"),
    ("watercolor",     "watercolor painting, soft pastel washes"),
    ("cyberpunk",      "cyberpunk neon, holographic, rain"),
]
for style_name, style_prompt in styles:
    sana_img2img(
        prompt=style_prompt,
        image_path=src,
        model="sana-sprint-i2i-1.6b-1024",
        strength=0.55, steps=2,
        seed=42, output_dir=f"./out/{style_name}",
    )

docs.title: Why this works
docs.text:
• Base image: full Sana-1.6B at 20 steps for compositional anchor
  • Restyles: Sana-Sprint Img2Img at 2 steps — ~10× cheaper
  • strength=0.55 keeps layout but allows aesthetic shift
  • Same seed ensures all variants share latent structure

docs.title: Result on Thor
docs.text: We did this in the Gallery — 9.3s per restyle after warmup.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text: Pick a recipe based on what you want to build.

docs.title: Single image, default settings
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_generate
sana_generate(prompt="a serene koi pond")

docs.title: Fast inference (Sprint)
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_sprint_generate
sana_sprint_generate(prompt="...", model="sana-sprint-1.6b-1024", steps=2)

docs.title: Video clip (5s)
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_video_generate
sana_video_generate(prompt="...", frames=121, steps=15)

docs.title: Animate a still
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_image_to_video
sana_image_to_video(image_path="src.png", prompt="zoom out")

docs.title: Restyle an existing image
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_img2img
sana_img2img(prompt="anime style", image_path="src.png", strength=0.5)

docs.title: Best of K
docs.text: → Best-of-K example

docs.title: Multi-style batch
docs.text: → Multi-style example

docs.title: Full video pipeline
docs.text: → Video pipeline

docs.title: In-repo examples
docs.text:
The examples/ directory has 17 runnable scripts:

01_basic_generate.py        # sana_generate
02_list_models.py           # sana_load_model("list")
03_batch.py                 # sana_batch
04_direct_pipeline.py       # SanaPipelineWrapper directly
05_sprint.py                # sana_sprint_generate
06_pag.py                   # PAG variant
07_controlnet.py            # ControlNet
08_lora.py                  # sana_load_lora
09_low_vram.py              # sana_set_memory_mode("low")
10_comfyui_export.py        # sana_export_comfyui_workflow
11_inference_scale.py       # sana_inference_scale
12_scheduler.py             # sana_set_scheduler
13_video.py                 # generate hero assets (used to make this site)
14_image_to_video.py        # sana_image_to_video
15_train_lora.py            # sana_train_lora
16_train_video.py           # sana_train_video / longsana
17_solrl.py                 # sana_train_solrl

docs.title: Video Pipeline
docs.text:
Full pipeline: generate hero image → animate → score → output.

from strands_sana import (
    sana_generate, sana_image_to_video, sana_metric_clip, sana_clear_cache,
)

PROMPT = "a serene koi pond at sunrise, cherry blossoms drifting"

# 1. Generate base image with best quality
sana_clear_cache()
img = sana_generate(prompt=PROMPT, model="sana-1.5-1.6b-1024",
                    steps=20, seed=42, output_dir="./out")

# 2. Score it for sanity
score = sana_metric_clip(image_path=img["path"], prompt=PROMPT)
print(f"CLIP alignment: {score['score']:.3f}")

if score["score"] < 0.30:
    print("Re-rolling...")
    # could fall through to inference_scale here
else:
    # 3. Animate
    sana_clear_cache()
    video = sana_image_to_video(
        image_path=img["path"],
        prompt=PROMPT + ", soft camera drift",
        model="sana-video-i2v-480",
        frames=121, steps=15, seed=42,
        output_dir="./out",
    )
    print("Final video:", video["path"])

docs.title: Strands agent variant
docs.text:
from strands import Agent
from strands_sana import (
    sana_generate, sana_image_to_video, sana_metric_clip, sana_clear_cache,
)

agent = Agent(tools=[
    sana_generate, sana_image_to_video, sana_metric_clip, sana_clear_cache,
])

agent("""
Generate an image of a serene koi pond at sunrise. Score it with CLIP.
If alignment is above 0.3, animate it as a 5-second clip. Otherwise re-roll.
""")

The agent figures out the right tool sequence on its own.

docs.title: Your first image
docs.text: This walkthrough produces the hero image you see on the home page.

docs.title: Code
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_generate

result = sana_generate(
    prompt=("a majestic rubber duck wearing aviator goggles, "
            "perched on a futuristic motorcycle in a cyberpunk neon-lit city, "
            "raining, photorealistic, 8k, dramatic cinematic lighting"),
    model="sana-1.5-1.6b-1024",
    steps=20,
    seed=42,
    output_dir="./out",
)
print(result)

docs.title: What happens
docs.text:
1. Pipeline cache miss → downloads Efficient-Large-Model/SANA1.5_1.6B_1024px_diffusers (~10 GB) on first run.
  2. Loads the pipeline on best available device (cuda → mps → cpu).
  3. Runs 20 denoising steps with flow_match_euler scheduler.
  4. Saves PNG to ./out/sana_<timestamp>.png.
{
  "status": "success",
  "path": "./out/sana_1779038153007.png",
  "model": "sana-1.5-1.6b-1024",
  "kind": "t2i",
  "prompt": "...",
  "width": 1024,
  "height": 1024,
  "count": 1
}

docs.title: Result
docs.text: → Want it 10× faster? See Sana-Sprint.

docs.title: Your first video
docs.text: This produces the cyberpunk teaser at the top of the homepage.

docs.title: Code
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_video_generate

result = sana_video_generate(
    prompt=("neon rain falling on the streets of a cyberpunk city, "
            "reflections in puddles, holographic billboards"),
    model="sana-video-2b-480",
    steps=10,        # fast
    frames=49,       # ~2s @ 24fps
    seed=99,
    output_dir="./out",
)
print(result)

docs.title: What you get
docs.text:
{
  "status": "success",
  "path": "./out/sana_video_<ts>.mp4",
  "model": "sana-video-2b-480",
  "frames": 49,
  "fps": 24,
  "duration_s": 2.04
}

docs.title: MP4 encoding
docs.text:
For real MP4 output (instead of WebP fallback), install the [video] extra:

pip install 'strands-sana[video]'   # imageio + imageio-ffmpeg

Without it, strands-sana writes animated WebP — works everywhere but harder to share.

→ Next: Image-to-video to animate a still.

docs.title: Basic install
docs.text:
pip install strands-sana

This pulls in diffusers, torch, transformers, accelerate, pyyaml.

docs.title: With GPU acceleration
docs.text:
You'll want a CUDA-compatible torch wheel for non-trivial inference:

# CUDA 12.1
pip install torch>=2.4 --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121

pip install strands-sana

On Apple Silicon, MPS works out of the box — no extra wheel needed.

docs.title: Optional extras
docs.text:
pip install 'strands-sana[hf]'           # HF Hub + safetensors
pip install 'strands-sana[video]'        # imageio + imageio-ffmpeg (MP4 export)
pip install 'strands-sana[lora]'         # peft for LoRA support
pip install 'strands-sana[quantization]' # bitsandbytes + optimum-quanto
pip install 'strands-sana[dev]'          # pytest, ruff, black, mypy
pip install 'strands-sana[docs]'         # mkdocs-material

docs.title: With training support
docs.text:
Training tools shell out to upstream NVlabs/Sana scripts. Either:

git clone https://github.com/NVlabs/Sana.git
# Tools auto-detect ./Sana

…or set SANA_ROOT:

export SANA_ROOT=/path/to/Sana

docs.title: Verify install
docs.text:
import strands_sana
print(strands_sana.__version__)

from strands_sana import sana_load_model
print(sana_load_model(model="list"))

You should see 15 models registered.

docs.title: Hardware requirements
docs.text:
Model VRAM (default) VRAM (mode='low') Sana 0.6B 4 GB 2 GB Sana 1.6B / 1.5 1.6B 8 GB 4 GB Sana 1.5 4.8B 16 GB 8 GB Sana 1.6B @ 4K 22 GB 8 GB SANA-Video 2B 12 GB 6 GB LongSANA 20 GB 10 GB

CPU-only works for tiny experiments but is slow (~5 min/image).

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
Install the package, run a script, get a PNG. ~2 minutes once weights are cached.

pip install strands-sana

docs.title: As a Strands Agent
docs.text:
from strands import Agent
from strands_sana import sana_generate

agent = Agent(tools=[sana_generate])
result = agent("Generate a cyberpunk duck on a motorcycle, 1024x1024")

The agent reads the prompt, picks sana-1.6b-1024, downloads weights on first use, runs inference, and returns the saved path.

docs.title: Direct call (no agent)
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_generate

result = sana_generate(
    prompt="a cyberpunk duck on a motorcycle",
    model="sana-1.6b-1024",
    steps=20,
    seed=42,
    output_dir="./out",
)
print(result["path"])

docs.title: Pick a faster model
docs.text:
# Sana-Sprint: 1-2 step distilled, ~0.1s per image on H100
from strands_sana import sana_sprint_generate

sana_sprint_generate(
    prompt="a cyberpunk duck on a motorcycle",
    model="sana-sprint-1.6b-1024",
    steps=2,
)

docs.title: Generate a video
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_video_generate

sana_video_generate(
    prompt="a serene sunrise over misty mountains",
    model="sana-video-2b-480",
    steps=15,
    frames=121,  # 5s @ 24fps
    seed=7,
)

→ Continue: First image · First video

docs.title: ComfyUI Workflow Export
docs.text:
Generate a JSON workflow file you can drop into ComfyUI (with the Sana node pack) to reproduce a strands-sana run as a visual graph.

from strands_sana import sana_export_comfyui_workflow

sana_export_comfyui_workflow(
    prompt="a serene koi pond, ink wash painting",
    output_path="./koi_workflow.json",
    model="sana-1.6b-1024",
    width=1024, height=1024,
    steps=20, cfg=4.5, seed=1234,
)
# Open koi_workflow.json in ComfyUI → Load

docs.title: Generated graph
docs.text:
graph LR
    Loader["1: CheckpointLoaderSimpleSana"] -->|model| KSampler["5: KSampler"]
    Loader -->|clip| Pos["2: CLIPTextEncode (positive)"]
    Loader -->|clip| Neg["3: CLIPTextEncode (negative)"]
    Pos -->|conditioning| KSampler
    Neg -->|conditioning| KSampler
    Empty["4: EmptyLatentImage"] --> KSampler
    KSampler --> Decode["6: VAEDecode"]
    Loader -->|vae| Decode
    Decode --> Save["7: SaveImage"]

docs.title: Use cases
docs.text:
• 🎬 Hand off to artists who use ComfyUI
  • 🔬 Reproduce a strands-sana run in a different ecosystem
  • 🛠️ Visualize what your tool calls look like as a graph
  • 📦 Build hybrid workflows (start in agent, finish in ComfyUI)

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
• The generated workflow is generic — doesn't include LoRA / ControlNet / scheduler swaps
  • For full fidelity to a strands-sana run, log the JSON returned by each tool call

docs.title: ControlNet
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_controlnet_generate

sana_controlnet_generate(
    prompt="a futuristic robot",
    control_image="hed_edges.png",
    model="<a Sana-ControlNet checkpoint>",
    controlnet_conditioning_scale=0.8,
    steps=20,
)

Diffusers ControlNet weights are 'Coming soon' upstream

As of NVlabs/Sana model_zoo.md, the diffusers-format ControlNet checkpoints are listed as "Coming soon". Native checkpoints exist (e.g. Efficient-Large-Model/Sana_1600M_1024px_BF16_ControlNet_HED) but require upstream's app/sana_controlnet_pipeline.py loader.

The tool detects this and returns a helpful error pointing to the workaround.

docs.title: When upstream ships diffusers ControlNet
docs.text:
Just point at the new repo:

sana_controlnet_generate(
    model="Efficient-Large-Model/Sana_1600M_1024px_BF16_ControlNet_HED_diffusers",
    prompt="...", control_image="...",
)

docs.title: Control image preprocessing
docs.text:
ControlNet expects pre-processed control maps (HED edges, Canny, depth, normal). Use upstream's HED preprocessor or any ControlNet-compatible preprocessor:

import cv2
img = cv2.Canny(cv2.imread("source.png"), 100, 200)
cv2.imwrite("canny.png", img)

docs.title: controlnet_conditioning_scale
docs.text: value effect 0.0 ControlNet disabled 0.5 gentle guidance 1.0 default — strong guidance 1.5+ over-saturated, may break

docs.title: Image Generation
docs.text:
The core tool is sana_generate. It auto-dispatches to PAG when pag_scale > 0, handles aspect-ratio binning, and supports up to 4K with VAE tiling.

sana_generate(prompt: str, ...) -> dict

docs.title: Basic call
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_generate

result = sana_generate(
    prompt="a serene koi pond, ink wash painting",
    model="sana-1.6b-1024",
    steps=20,
    seed=42,
)

docs.title: All parameters
docs.text: arg type default notes prompt str required text description negative_prompt str "" what to avoid model str? None alias from registry width / height int? model resolution snapped to bucket steps int? per-model (20 default) denoising steps guidance_scale float? per-model (4.5) CFG strength seed int? None reproducibility pag_scale float 0.0 >0 → SanaPAGPipeline use_complex_instruction bool False prepend Gemma-2 prompt expander use_resolution_binning bool True snap to trained bucket num_images int 1 per prompt output_dir str? ./generated save directory

docs.title: Multi-image batch
docs.text:
sana_generate(
    prompt="a clay figurine of a frog",
    model="sana-0.6b-512",
    num_images=4,
    seed=20,
)
# Returns: { "paths": [path1, path2, path3, path4], "count": 4, ... }

docs.title: Multiple prompts
docs.text:
For different prompts, use sana_batch:

from strands_sana import sana_batch

sana_batch(
    prompts=["a duck", "a frog", "a cat"],
    model="sana-0.6b-512",
    seed=42,
)

docs.title: Different aspect ratios
docs.text:
sana_generate(prompt="a cinematic landscape",
              width=1280, height=768, model="sana-1.6b-1024")

use_resolution_binning=True (default) snaps to the closest trained bucket — for Sana-1024 buckets are e.g. (1024, 1024), (1280, 768), (768, 1280), etc.

docs.title: With Gemma-2 prompt expansion
docs.text:
Sana uses a Gemma-2 LLM as text encoder. Pass use_complex_instruction=True to prepend the official prompt-expansion template — short prompts get fleshed out automatically.

sana_generate(
    prompt="a duck",   # short
    model="sana-1.6b-1024",
    use_complex_instruction=True,  # expanded server-side via Gemma-2
)

docs.title: Image-to-Image (sana_img2img)
docs.text:
Restyle an existing image at Sana-Sprint speed.

from strands_sana import sana_img2img

sana_img2img(
    prompt="anime cel-shaded style, vibrant colors",
    image_path="source.png",
    model="sana-sprint-i2i-1.6b-1024",
    strength=0.6,    # 0..1: how much to change
    steps=2,         # Sprint sweet spot for img2img is 2-4
    seed=42,
)

docs.title: How strength works
docs.text: strength effect 0.0 unchanged source 0.3 subtle restyle, preserves layout 0.5 balanced 0.7 strong reinterpretation 1.0 full regeneration (ignores source)

docs.title: Demo
docs.text: Source strength=0.5"anime cel-shaded" strength=0.6"oil painting"

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Sprint Img2Img doesn't accept negative_prompt or CFG (model is distilled)
  • For more denoising control, use full Sana with manual init noise (advanced)
  • 2 steps is fastest; 4 steps gives better fidelity

docs.title: Inference Scaling
docs.text:
Generate K candidates, pick the best by CLIP score (or NVILA when ready).

from strands_sana import sana_inference_scale

result = sana_inference_scale(
    prompt="a cherry blossom branch in soft moonlight",
    model="sana-1.6b-1024",
    n_samples=4,
    score_fn="clip",    # or "first" to skip scoring
    seed_start=200,
)

print(result["best"])   # best candidate path + score
print(result["scores"]) # all 4 scores

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
graph LR
    P[Prompt] --> G[Generate K with seeds]
    G --> S[Score each with CLIP]
    S --> B[Return best + all]

    style B fill:#5e35b1,stroke:#333,color:#fff

docs.title: Demo — 4 candidates
docs.text: seed 200CLIP 0.431 seed 201CLIP 0.453 seed 202CLIP 0.462 ← winner seed 203CLIP 0.459

docs.title: When to use
docs.text:
• ✅ Production where quality matters more than latency
  • ✅ Difficult prompts (compositional, off-distribution)
  • ✅ Building a "best-of-N" agent loop
  • ❌ Real-time UX (latency is K× single inference)

docs.title: Score functions
docs.text:
score_fn Method Speed "clip" OpenAI CLIP-vit-base-patch32 ~0.5s/image "first" No scoring, return seed_start 0s

For more sophisticated scoring (NVILA-2B, ImageReward, MPS), add a custom score_fn — see API reference.

docs.title: LoRA Adapters
docs.text:
Load and unload LoRA weights at runtime to specialize a base Sana model.

from strands_sana import sana_load_lora, sana_generate, sana_unload_loras

sana_load_lora(
    repo_or_path="user/sana-yarnart-lora",
    scale=0.8,
    model="sana-1.6b-1024",
)

sana_generate(prompt="a duck in yarn art style",
              model="sana-1.6b-1024", steps=15)

sana_unload_loras(model="sana-1.6b-1024")

docs.title: Requires PEFT
docs.text:
pip install 'strands-sana[lora]'
# OR: pip install peft

docs.title: Stacking multiple LoRAs
docs.text:
sana_load_lora("user/style-lora", scale=0.6, adapter_name="style")
sana_load_lora("user/character-lora", scale=0.7, adapter_name="char")
sana_generate(prompt="...")

The wrapper tracks active adapters in pipe._loaded_loras.

docs.title: Sources
docs.text:
• HuggingFace Hub: user/repo format
  • Local path: /path/to/lora.safetensors
  • Train your own: see Training

docs.title: Train your own LoRA
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_train_lora

sana_train_lora(
    instance_data_dir="./my-photos",
    instance_prompt="a photo of sks dog",
    max_train_steps=500,
    num_processes=4,    # 4 GPUs
    dry_run=False,      # actually launch
)

→ Full training guide: Training

docs.title: Memory & Quantization
docs.text: Run Sana on smaller GPUs. The biggest model (Sana-1.5 4.8B) normally needs 16 GB; with mode='low' it fits in 8 GB.

docs.title: Memory modes
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_set_memory_mode

sana_set_memory_mode(mode="low", model="sana-1.6b-1024")
mode What it enables When to use "fast" Nothing extra — full GPU residence A100/H100, plenty of VRAM "balanced" model_cpu_offload Default for CUDA "low" sequential_cpu_offload + vae_tiling + attention_slicing 8-12 GB VRAM "ultra-low" low + future int4 quant 4-8 GB VRAM (with [quantization])

docs.title: Quantization
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_quantize

# Auto-probe: tries quanto → bitsandbytes → nunchaku
sana_quantize(bits=4, backend="auto", model="sana-1.6b-1024")
Backend Install Status quanto pip install optimum-quanto runtime quant bitsandbytes pip install bitsandbytes requires reload-with-config nunchaku pip install nunchaku (SVDQuant 4-bit) pre-quantized weights 
# Bundle install:
pip install 'strands-sana[quantization]'

docs.title: VAE swap (DC-AE-Lite)
docs.text:
Switch to a more efficient VAE without retraining the diffusion backbone:

from strands_sana import sana_swap_vae

sana_swap_vae(
    vae_repo="mit-han-lab/dc-ae-lite-f32c32-sana-1.1-diffusers",
    model="sana-1.6b-1024",
)

DC-AE-Lite is ~30% faster decode with similar reconstruction quality.

docs.title: Cache management
docs.text:
Free GPU memory between runs:

from strands_sana import sana_clear_cache

sana_clear_cache()
# {"freed_pipelines": 3}

docs.title: Hardware suggestions
docs.text: GPU Recommended config RTX 4090 (24 GB) mode="fast" + Sana-1.5 4.8B RTX 4070 Ti (12 GB) mode="balanced" + Sana-1.6B RTX 3060 (12 GB) mode="low" + Sana-0.6B or Sprint RTX 2060 (8 GB) mode="low" + 4-bit quant + Sprint Apple M-series mode="balanced", MPS auto-detected CPU only mode="balanced", expect ~5min/image

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
Score generated images programmatically.

from strands_sana import sana_metric_clip, sana_metric_imagereward

sana_metric_clip(image_path="duck.png",
                 prompt="a happy rubber duck on a motorcycle")
# {"score": 0.46, "metric": "clip-score"}

sana_metric_imagereward(image_path="duck.png", prompt="...")
# {"score": 1.34, "metric": "image-reward"}  # higher = better

docs.title: CLIPScore
docs.text:
OpenAI CLIP-vit-base-patch32 cosine similarity. Range 0..1, higher = better text-image alignment.

  • ✅ Fast (~0.5s)
  • ✅ Built-in to [hf] extra
  • ❌ Not perfect — known biases (favors detail-rich images)

docs.title: ImageReward
docs.text:
pip install image-reward

Trained on human preferences. More expensive (~2s) but better correlated with quality.

docs.title: Use in inference scaling
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_inference_scale

# Built-in CLIP scoring:
sana_inference_scale(prompt="...", n_samples=8, score_fn="clip")

For custom scorers, see Inference Scaling.

docs.title: Future: full upstream metrics
docs.text:
Upstream NVlabs/Sana ships:

  • GenEval — compositional generation benchmark
  • DPG-Bench — Dense Prompt Graph benchmark
  • FID — Frechet Inception Distance
  • CLIP-T — text alignment

Wiring these in as sana_metric_* is on the v0.5 roadmap. For now, use upstream's evaluation scripts directly.

docs.title: Models
docs.text: strands-sana registers 15 checkpoints across 7 pipeline kinds.

docs.title: List at runtime
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_load_model

print(sana_load_model(model="list"))
print(sana_load_model(model="list", kind="video"))      # only video
print(sana_load_model(model="list", tag="sana-1.5"))    # only Sana-1.5

docs.title: Image models
docs.text: Alias Pipeline Resolution Params Use case sana-0.6b-512 t2i 512 590M Fast iteration sana-0.6b-1024 t2i 1024 590M Lightweight 1024 sana-1.6b-1024 t2i 1024 1.6B Default sana-1.6b-multiling t2i 1024 1.6B Chinese/Emoji sana-1.5-1.6b-1024 t2i 1024 1.6B Improved GenEval sana-1.5-4.8b-1024 t2i 1024 4.8B Top quality sana-1.6b-2k t2i 2048 1.6B 2K resolution sana-1.6b-4k t2i 4096 1.6B 4K resolution sana-sprint-0.6b-1024 sprint 1024 590M 1-2 step, ultra-fast sana-sprint-1.6b-1024 sprint 1024 1.6B 1-2 step, top quality sana-sprint-i2i-1.6b-1024 sprint-i2i 1024 1.6B Image-to-image

docs.title: Video models
docs.text: Alias Pipeline Resolution Frames Use case sana-video-2b-480 video 480 121 Default 5s clip sana-video-2b-720 video 720 121 720p with LTX-VAE longsana-2b-480 video 480 720 Real-time minute-long sana-video-i2v-480 image-to-video 480 121 Animate a still

docs.title: Pipeline kinds
docs.text:
graph TB
    Wrapper["SanaPipelineWrapper"] --> T2I["t2i → SanaPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> PAG["pag → SanaPAGPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> Sprint["sprint → SanaSprintPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> SprintI2I["sprint-i2i → SanaSprintImg2ImgPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> Ctrl["controlnet → SanaControlNetPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> Vid["video → SanaVideoPipeline"]
    Wrapper --> I2V["image-to-video → SanaImageToVideoPipeline"]

docs.title: Per-model defaults
docs.text:
Each model has tuned default_steps, default_guidance, default_frames. The wrapper uses these unless you override:

from strands_sana.models import get_model_info

info = get_model_info("sana-sprint-1.6b-1024")
print(info.default_steps)     # 2 — Sprint sweet spot
print(info.default_guidance)  # 0.0 — distilled, no CFG

docs.title: Pre-download a checkpoint
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_prefetch_model

sana_prefetch_model(model="sana-1.6b-1024")
# Returns: { "local_path": "~/.cache/huggingface/hub/..." }

docs.title: PAG — Perturbed Attention Guidance
docs.text:
PAG (Perturbed Attention Guidance) is a CFG-style guidance technique that improves quality without text conditioning. It costs ~2× compute.

from strands_sana import sana_generate

sana_generate(
    prompt="a stained glass dragon",
    model="sana-1.5-1.6b-1024",
    pag_scale=2.0,        # > 0 → switches to SanaPAGPipeline
    steps=15,
    seed=42,
)

docs.title: How pag_scale interacts with guidance_scale
docs.text:
pag_scale guidance_scale Pipeline 0 any SanaPipeline (regular CFG) > 0 any SanaPAGPipeline (PAG + CFG)

Recommended: pag_scale=2.0, keep guidance_scale=4.5 (default).

docs.title: Demo
docs.text: Sana-1.5 1.6B + PAG (`pag_scale=2.0`) · 15 steps · 62s on Thor

docs.title: When to use PAG
docs.text:
• ✅ Subjects with intricate detail (jewelry, glass, fur)
  • ✅ Off-distribution prompts where CFG alone overshoots
  • ❌ Sprint models (already distilled)
  • ❌ Speed-critical pipelines (~2× compute)

docs.title: Schedulers
docs.text:
Swap denoising schedulers at runtime. 10 aliases mapped to diffusers classes.

from strands_sana import sana_set_scheduler, sana_list_schedulers

print(sana_list_schedulers())

sana_set_scheduler(name="dpm-solver", model="sana-1.6b-1024")

docs.title: Available schedulers
docs.text: Alias Diffusers class Notes flow-match-euler FlowMatchEulerDiscreteScheduler Default — best for Sana flow-match-heun FlowMatchHeunDiscreteScheduler Higher fidelity, 2× cost dpm-solver DPMSolverMultistepScheduler Fast, supports flow_sigmas dpm-solver-single DPMSolverSinglestepScheduler Even faster dpm-solver-cosine CosineDPMSolverMultistepScheduler Cosine schedule euler EulerDiscreteScheduler Lossy on flow models euler-ancestral EulerAncestralDiscreteScheduler Adds noise per step ddim DDIMScheduler Lossy on flow models deis DEISMultistepScheduler Fast heun HeunDiscreteScheduler Lossy on flow models

docs.title: Demo — same prompt, 4 schedulers
docs.text: flow-match-euler(default) dpm-solver(13× faster) euler(lossy) ddim(lossy)

docs.title: Flow-matching compatibility
docs.text:
Sana is a flow-matching model. Its scheduler config has prediction_type=flow_prediction.

  • ✅ FlowMatch* schedulers — native fit
  • ✅ DPMSolver* schedulers — accept use_flow_sigmas=True
  • ⚠️ Other schedulers — work but quality degrades (we strip flow_prediction automatically and warn)

This was BUG #9 — caught on Thor.

docs.title: Speed comparison (Thor, sana-0.6b-1024, 10 steps)
docs.text: Scheduler Time flow-match-euler 48s (first run, ~12s warm) dpm-solver 13.3s euler 6.2s euler-ancestral 6.3s ddim 7.0s deis 7.1s heun 7.7s

docs.title: Sana-Sprint
docs.text:
Distilled 1-2 step inference. ~0.1s per 1024px image on H100, ~0.3s on RTX 4090.

from strands_sana import sana_sprint_generate

sana_sprint_generate(
    prompt="a cyberpunk hummingbird",
    model="sana-sprint-1.6b-1024",   # or sana-sprint-0.6b-1024
    steps=2,                          # sweet spot
    seed=42,
)

docs.title: How it works
docs.text:
Sprint uses sCM-LADD distillation:

  • sCM — continuous-time consistency distillation
  • LADD — Latent Adversarial Diffusion Distillation

The student model is trained to take giant denoising steps. Result: 1-2 steps gets the same quality as 20 steps of the teacher.

docs.title: Step count
docs.text:
Sprint accepts steps ∈ {1, 2, 4, 8, ...}.

steps speed quality 1 fastest sometimes blurry 2 default best balance 4 slower slight quality bump 8 slowest diminishing returns

Implementation note

Sprint's intermediate_timesteps=1.3 default is only valid for steps=2. The wrapper passes intermediate_timesteps=None automatically when steps≠2. (Caught by BUG #5.)

docs.title: Sprint Img2Img
docs.text:
Sprint's image-to-image variant uses the same checkpoint:

from strands_sana import sana_img2img

sana_img2img(
    prompt="anime cel-shaded style",
    image_path="src.png",
    model="sana-sprint-i2i-1.6b-1024",
    strength=0.5, steps=2,
)

→ See sana_img2img.

docs.title: Demo
docs.text:
Same prompt, same seed, two models:

Sana-1.5 1.6B20 steps · 6.4s

Sprint 1.6B2 steps · 0.6s

docs.title: Training
docs.text: strands-sana exposes the full upstream training stack as @tool functions. All training tools shell out to NVlabs/Sana scripts via subprocess.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
You need the upstream Sana repo. Either:

git clone https://github.com/NVlabs/Sana.git    # auto-detected

Or:

export SANA_ROOT=/path/to/Sana

docs.title: Available training jobs
docs.text: Tool Wraps Use case sana_train_lora train_dreambooth_lora_sana.py DreamBooth + LoRA sana_train train_scripts/train.py Full pretrain / finetune sana_train_scm_ladd train_scripts/train_scm_ladd.py Sana-Sprint distillation sana_train_solrl sol_rl/run_sana_single_node_8gpu.sh RL post-training (NVFP4 + BF16) sana_train_video train_video_ivjoint.py SANA-Video FSDP sana_train_longsana train_longsana.py Real-time minute-long video sana_list_training_configs n/a List 38 upstream configs

docs.title: Dry-run first
docs.text:
All training tools default to dry_run=True — they print the command without launching.

from strands_sana import sana_train_lora

result = sana_train_lora(
    instance_data_dir="./my-photos",
    instance_prompt="a photo of sks dog",
    max_train_steps=500,
    num_processes=4,
)
print(result["command"])
# accelerate launch --num_processes=4 train_scripts/train_dreambooth_lora_sana.py ...

docs.title: Launch for real
docs.text:
result = sana_train_lora(..., dry_run=False)
print(result["pid"])  # background process started

The function returns immediately. Tail the logs at output/<run-name>/.

docs.title: Pipeline overview
docs.text:
graph TB
    subgraph SFT["Supervised Fine-tuning"]
        LORA["sana_train_lora<br/>DreamBooth + LoRA"]
        FULL["sana_train<br/>Full pretrain/finetune"]
    end

    subgraph DISTILL["Distillation"]
        SCM["sana_train_scm_ladd<br/>Make a Sprint model"]
    end

    subgraph RL["RL"]
        SOLRL["sana_train_solrl<br/>NVFP4 rollout + BF16"]
    end

    subgraph VIDEO["Video"]
        VID["sana_train_video<br/>SANA-Video FSDP"]
        LONG["sana_train_longsana<br/>Minute-long real-time"]
    end

    Base[Sana base model] --> LORA
    Base --> FULL
    LORA --> SCM
    FULL --> SCM
    SCM --> SOLRL
    Base --> VID
    VID --> LONG

    style LORA fill:#5e35b1,stroke:#333,color:#fff
    style SOLRL fill:#ec407a,stroke:#333,color:#fff
    style LONG fill:#7e57c2,stroke:#333,color:#fff

docs.title: Example: LoRA on your own photos
docs.text:
sana_train_lora(
    instance_data_dir="./photos-of-my-cat",
    instance_prompt="a photo of sks cat",
    validation_prompt="a photo of sks cat in a yarn art style",
    pretrained_model="Efficient-Large-Model/Sana_1600M_1024px_BF16_diffusers",
    resolution=1024,
    train_batch_size=1,
    gradient_accumulation_steps=4,
    learning_rate=1e-4,
    max_train_steps=500,
    use_8bit_adam=True,
    mixed_precision="bf16",
    seed=0,
    num_processes=4,
    push_to_hub=True,
    hub_repo="cagataycali/sana-cat-lora",
    dry_run=False,
)

15-20 photos and 500 steps is enough for DreamBooth. ~30 min on a single A100.

docs.title: Example: Sol-RL post-training
docs.text:
sana_train_solrl(
    config_spec="configs/sol_rl/sana.py:sana_diffusionnft_pickscore",
    nproc_per_node=8,
    cuda_visible_devices="0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7",
    dry_run=False,
)

Sol-RL uses NVFP4 rollout + BF16 training for 4.6× faster RL convergence than standard diffusion-RL methods.

docs.title: Example: video FSDP training
docs.text:
sana_train_video(
    config_path="configs/sana_video_config/Sana_2000M_480px_AdamW_fsdp.yaml",
    num_processes=8,         # 8 GPUs
    chunk=False,              # set True for long-context training
    dry_run=False,
)

docs.title: List all configs
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_list_training_configs

result = sana_list_training_configs()
for cfg in result["configs"]:
    print(cfg)
# 38 configs across sana_config, sana_sprint_config, sana_video_config, sol_rl, ...

docs.title: Video Generation
docs.text: Two tools cover all video use cases: text-to-video (sana_video_generate) and image-to-video (sana_image_to_video).

docs.title: Text-to-Video
docs.text:
from strands_sana import sana_video_generate

sana_video_generate(
    prompt="a slow cinematic pan over snowy mountains, golden hour",
    model="sana-video-2b-480",   # 480p · 5s clips
    steps=15,
    frames=121,                   # ~5s @ 24fps
    seed=42,
)

docs.title: Models for video
docs.text: Model Resolution Default frames Notes sana-video-2b-480 480 121 (5s) Default — best speed/quality sana-video-2b-720 720 121 (5s) 720p with LTX-VAE refiner longsana-2b-480 480 720 (~27s @ 27fps) Real-time minute-long

docs.title: Output formats
docs.text:
sana_video_generate writes:

  1. MP4 if you have imageio-ffmpeg (pip install 'strands-sana[video]')
  2. Animated WebP as fallback (no extra deps)
result = sana_video_generate(prompt="...", frames=49)
# {
#   "path": "./generated/sana_video_<ts>.mp4",
#   "frames": 49, "fps": 24, "duration_s": 2.04,
# }

docs.title: Image-to-Video
docs.text:
Animate a still:

from strands_sana import sana_image_to_video

sana_image_to_video(
    image_path="hero.png",
    prompt="zoom out slowly, leaves swirling in the wind",
    model="sana-video-i2v-480",
    frames=49,
    seed=42,
)

The source image is encoded into the first frame's latent, then the diffusion model unrolls subsequent frames.

docs.title: Resolution constraints
docs.text:
SANA-Video requires (height, width) divisible by 32. The wrapper snaps automatically and disables binning to ensure your sizes are honored.

sana_video_generate(prompt="...", height=480, width=480)  # OK
sana_video_generate(prompt="...", height=300)             # → snaps to 288

docs.title: Long videos
docs.text:
For minute-long real-time clips:

sana_video_generate(
    prompt="a calm zen garden, soft camera drift",
    model="longsana-2b-480",
    frames=720,    # 27s @ 27fps
    steps=4,       # LongSANA is distilled — few steps
)
